goal_scope: Add a higher-functionality browser-local missed-day Quick Restart workflow to the public-safe LifeMax app and deploy it to Vercel.
truth_boundary: This proof covers browser-local demo behavior only.
No WHOOP, account, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, Postgres, backend write, reminder send, or recovery-state mutation is connected or claimed.

Production deployment was previously approved in this goal with PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_APPROVED=true promote; no credential, auth setting, DNS/custom-domain, backend, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, Postgres, or WHOOP mutation was performed.
The latest LifeMax lab preflight and boot packet remain red, so broader shared-context/daily-usability readiness is not complete.
product_gap_evidence: docs/LIFEMAX_HIGH_FUNCTIONING_ACCEPTANCE_TEST_MATRIX.md Wave 2 calls for missed-day re-entry: 1 missed day quick restart asks minimum fields, 3 missed days asks what changed, and 7 missed days has a reset path.
A live text probe against https://life-max-omega.vercel.app/today before this repair returned hasRestart:false while rescue/privacy were present.
The prior verifier said full backend/shared-context completion was not justified; this slice keeps improving the public browser-local app while preserving that boundary.
defects_found: Today had no visible missed-day restart or re-entry workflow, so the app still behaved like a same-day demo after a gap.
First Playwright rerun found the restart priority appeared in multiple command-center cells, making the assertion and accessibility weak.
Second rerun found the expected command copy changed after restart lowered intensity; the test had to reflect the intentional protective state.
Third rerun found a loose State selector matched recovery-state copy; the assertion was tightened.
Fourth rerun found generic saved text became ambiguous after adding Restart; assertions now use explicit status tile labels.

repairs_made.summary: Added QuickRestart types, storage normalization, export summary restart status, derived restart_summary, day-review evidence contribution, and quick restart risk flag.

Added Quick Restart panel with missed window, energy, sleep, one priority, what changed, and reminder boundary; saving creates a conservative check-in, one-priority plan, lowered intensity, and local memory candidate. Added Profile summary and Privacy copy.
Added restrained restart-panel styling consistent with the existing app shell.
Added unit and Playwright coverage for quick restart derivation, export summary, save flow, refresh persistence, Profile/export visibility, Privacy copy, and ambiguous-selector regressions.

local_checks.summary: tsc --noEmit passed after quick restart model/UI updates.
eslint passed after quick restart model/UI updates.
Reruns exposed ambiguous selectors and intentional lowered-intensity copy after restart; after repairs, Next build plus Playwright visual smoke passed: 2 tests.
http://127.0.0.1:3000/today exposed Quick Restart, local-only/no-recovery-state-mutation copy, width 390/390, and no serious browser errors.
No whitespace errors in the final code diff.

browser_flows_proven: Today exposes a Quick Restart panel above the daily plan, not hidden in Profile or docs.
User can choose missed window, energy, sleep, one restart priority, what changed, and reminder boundary.
Saving restart creates visible local state: Restart saved, lowered-intensity command center, conservative check-in, one-priority plan, and local memory candidate.
Restart state and form values persist after refresh through localStorage.
Profile shows Restart: saved and export preview includes quick_restart plus the restart note.
Privacy is reachable and names missed-day quick restart as browser-local data.
The copy says no reminder, Telegram message, backend write, or recovery-state mutation is sent.
Mobile viewport 390px had no horizontal overflow locally and on both production aliases.

git.proof_commit_note: This proof packet is written after the deployed code commit; final proof commit hash is reported in the Codex final response.

production_smoke.script_summary: Playwright shell smoke drove Today quick restart save, refresh persistence, Profile/export preview, and Privacy on both production aliases; no serious browser errors were captured.

independent_verification.note: quality_verifier inspected the Quick Restart proof, source, checks, Vercel aliases, live production behavior, and lab boot truth. It passed this as a public browser-local checkpoint and explicitly rejected broader COMPLETE_OK because shared-context/backend/WHOOP/Telegram/n8n/public MCP/Grok/Postgres remain red or unproved.
commands_rerun_by_verifier: fresh Playwright live smoke on life-max-omega and lifemax-web
vercel inspect for both production aliases
verifier_findings: Quick Restart was visible on Today, saved successfully, persisted after refresh, and appeared in Profile/export preview and Privacy on both production aliases.

The broader acceptance flow on omega also passed: Today state changes, Midday Rescue, Evening Close, Capture, Patterns review, Experiment observation persistence, mobile and desktop width, and no runtime errors.
Privacy boundary is honest; exact no-reminder/no-recovery-state-mutation copy appears on the Today Quick Restart panel rather than the Privacy page.
Proof files were still uncommitted before this update.
disqualifying_gaps_for_complete: LifeMax lab boot/shared-context state remains red and safe_to_claim_daily_usable=false.
No current proof exists for WHOOP, accounts, backend/shared-context, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, Postgres, or daily shared-context usability.
get_life_safety_state and get_grok_context_brief are not loaded by a fresh green shared-context loop.

stop_continue_decision: checkpoint, not complete: this continuation adds and deploys browser-local missed-day re-entry, but full LifeMax shared-context/backend/daily-use readiness remains red and unproved.
next_action: Run independent verifier, commit and push proof; continue the broader goal with either another Wave 2 local workflow or the approval-gated shared-context/MCP repair path.
